codeReview.summary: The fix correctly addresses the bug where container_id was never stored after start(). The implementation uses Mutex<Option<String>> for interior mutability, and properly stores the container ID in start() and accesses it in all dependent methods (stop, exec_in_container, write_file, read_file, is_running). The fix is minimal, focused, and all validation passes.

issues.description: stop() does not reset container_id to None after killing the container. This means container_id() will still return Some(id) after stop() is called, which is misleading. Consider adding 'let mut guard = self.container_id.lock().unwrap(); *guard = None;' after the successful kill at line 464.

summary: Fix for tools-gvisor-support: container_id never stored after start(). The fix changes container_id from Option<String> to Mutex<Option<String>> for interior mutability, and properly stores the ID returned from docker run in start(). All dependent methods now correctly access the stored container_id via the mutex. Build, tests, and clippy all pass. One minor observation: stop() does not reset container_id to None after killing the container, but this is non-blocking since no subsequent operations should occur after stop() anyway.
